description_long: This object is a compact, double-barreled shotgun with a classic design, featuring a wooden stock and fore-end paired with a blued steel barrel assembly. The barrels are arranged side-by-side, and the overall length is notably short, suggesting a sawed-off configuration. The stock is full-sized and made of dark-stained wood, smoothly contoured for shoulder support. The metal receiver and barrels have a matte black finish, with the twin barrels clearly visible from the front. The trigger guard is rounded and encloses a single trigger, with no visible pump or lever mechanisms. The break-action hinge is implied by the geometry at the receiver-barrel junction, and the object is robustly built, typical of traditional shotguns.
description: A compact, side-by-side double-barrel shotgun with a wooden stock and matte black barrels, featuring a rounded trigger guard and break-action design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full wooden stock, receiver, and the twin barrels extending forward.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the two parallel barrels and the dark, blued steel finish.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the stock, trigger guard, and the break-action hinge area.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the butt of the wooden stock and the alignment of the barrels.
attribute_barrel_configuration: side-by-side double-barrel, no visible ribbing or venting, short barrel length
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, medium-sized trigger guard with smooth edges and no finger grooves
